stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers' and 'llama'.
The content points to the model's use case in text generation tasks.

stories.title: LLMs as Noisy Channels: A Shannon Perspective on Model Capacity and Scaling Laws
stories.author: Yiyuan Ma
bullet_points: Proposes the 'Shannon Scaling Law' to reframe LLM training as information transmission over a noisy channel.
Addresses limitations of current monotonic scaling laws by incorporating non-monotonic phenomena like catastrophic overtraining and quantization degradation.
Grounds the theoretical framework in the Shannon-Hartley theorem, offering a unified perspective on model capacity and scaling.

stories.title: IBM Spins Off the First Pure-Play Quantum Chip Foundry
stories.author: rbanffy
bullet_points: IBM is establishing a dedicated, pure-play foundry for quantum chips.
This move signals a significant industrial shift toward specialized quantum hardware manufacturing.
The establishment of a foundry addresses a critical bottleneck in the scaling and production of quantum computing technology.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation, indicating its primary function as a language model.
The associated tags point to its foundation in the transformers framework and use of safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates its use for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware for optimized performance.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects these apps to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all AI processing runs entirely on the user's local device for privacy.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run advanced coding assistants (like Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using local or cloud models.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
The partnership involves Ollama, OpenAI, and ROOST (Robust Open Online Safety Tools).

stories.title: Cox Media fined after bragging it spied on users through their phones
stories.author: Adi Robertson
bullet_points: Cox Media and associated marketing firms were fined by the FTC for deceptive practices.
The controversy centers on claims that the companies were secretly listening to users via phones and smart devices.
The fine targets the alleged misuse of user data and privacy violations.

stories.title: Hackers are learning to exploit chatbot ‘personalities’
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Hackers are developing methods to exploit the 'personalities' of AI chatbots.
The vulnerability represents an evolution from earlier, simpler forms of AI hacking.
This highlights growing security concerns surrounding advanced conversational AI models.

stories.title: Four Russian satellites are now within striking distance of an ICEYE radarsat
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: Russian satellites are reportedly close enough to engage an ICEYE radarsat.
The capability described is noted as unusual for satellites performing standard missions.
The story touches upon advanced satellite technology and potential military/surveillance implications.

stories.title: LLMs as Noisy Channels: A Shannon Perspective on Model Capacity and Scaling Laws
stories.author: Yiyuan Ma
bullet_points: Proposes the 'Shannon Scaling Law' to re-evaluate LLM scaling dynamics.
Challenges existing monotonic power laws by framing LLM training as information transmission over a noisy channel.
Aims to theoretically explain non-monotonic phenomena like catastrophic overtraining and quantization degradation.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a specific model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, trending on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text generation tasks, indicating its function as a generative AI.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the current LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for local generation on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to run tools like Claude Code within Ollama's ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary APIs like Anthropic's.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated via Ollama, can read, modify, and execute code locally.
The functionality supports various open-weight alternatives like gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Why Nuro thinks being a robotaxi ‘second mover’ gives it an advantage
stories.author: Andrew J. Hawkins
bullet_points: Waymo maintains a dominant lead in the robotaxi market with a large operational fleet.
Nuro is positioned as a 'second mover' in the competitive autonomous vehicle space.
The article explores the potential strategic advantages of being a follower rather than the first mover.

stories.title: US's big bet on quantum computing may not be entirely legal
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: The US government's significant investment in quantum computing faces potential legal hurdles.
The initiative includes the launch of a new quantum foundry company, prompting questions about its necessity.
The article suggests a critical evaluation is needed regarding the scope and legality of the national quantum strategy.

stories.title: SpaceX's Starship V3—still a work in progress—mostly successful on first flight
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: SpaceX's Starship V3 flight was largely successful in its initial test.
The vehicle is still considered a work in progress and has significant hurdles to overcome.
Full flight to low-Earth orbit remains a future goal for the Starship program.

stories.title: SkillOpt: Executive Strategy for Self-Evolving Agent Skills
stories.author: Chong Luo
bullet_points: SkillOpt proposes a new paradigm for developing AI agent skills, moving beyond current hand-crafted or loosely controlled evolution methods.
The core idea is to train the agent's skill as an external state, applying optimization discipline similar to weight-space optimization in deep learning.
The system aims to be the first systematic, controllable text-space optimizer for agent skills, addressing current limitations in reliable skill improvement under feedback.

stories.title: From Raw Experience to Skill Consumption: A Systematic Study of Model-Generated Agent Skills
stories.author: Chong Luo
bullet_points: Language agents are improving by reusing 'skills,' which are structured procedures distilled from past experience.
Domain-level and model-generated skills are highlighted as promising for fast, scalable adaptation within specific domains.
Despite proliferation of extraction methods, there is a lack of comprehensive understanding regarding these agent skills.

stories.title: ETCHR: Editing To Clarify and Harness Reasoning
stories.author: Dahua Lin
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitation of purely textual Chain-of-Thought reasoning in Multimodal LLMs for complex visual tasks.
It proposes 'ETCHR,' a novel approach that decouples reasoning from image generation by using a dedicated image editing model.
This method aims to overcome the constraints of fixed toolkits and the noise associated with unified multimodal methods for 'thinking with images.'

stories.title: Good Token Hunting: A Hitchhiker's Guide to Token Selection for Visual Geometry Transformers
stories.author: Igor Gilitschenski
bullet_points: Visual Geometry Transformers are powerful for 3D reconstruction but suffer from quadratic computational cost due to global attention.
The paper proposes a strategy called 'Good Token Hunting' to improve efficiency.
This method restricts the number of key/value tokens used by each query to enhance scalability.

stories.title: PGT: Procedurally Generated Tasks for improving visual grounding in MLLMs
stories.author: Adriana Romero-Soriano
bullet_points: Proposes Procedurally Generated Tasks (PGT) to enhance fine-grained visual understanding in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
PGT works by overlaying unambiguous geometric primitives onto images to generate dense supervision.
The framework also functions as a low-cost diagnostic tool to pinpoint the causes of MLLM perception failures.

stories.title: Human Decision-Making with Persuasive and Narrative LLM Explanations
stories.author: Murat Kantarcioglu
bullet_points: LLMs can enhance human decision-making in classification tasks beyond just accurate predictions.
The research focuses on the impact of LLM-generated narrative explanations on objective human judgment.
Prior work suggests people find AI narrative explanations understandable and convincing for changing beliefs.

stories.title: Leveraging Foundation Models for Causal Generative Modeling
stories.author: Xintao Wu
bullet_points: The research addresses the need for causal generative modeling to build reliable AI capable of counterfactual reasoning.
The proposed FM-CGM is a modular framework designed to integrate causal reasoning with pretrained foundation models.
It aims to unify existing approaches by leveraging the zero-shot reasoning capabilities of large foundation models for visual causal tasks.

stories.title: Complete-muE: Optimal Hyperparameter Transfer and Scaling for MoE Models
stories.author: Yan Kang
bullet_points: Introduces Complete-muE, a new framework for hyperparameter transfer in transformer models.
Specifically addresses the challenges of transferring hyperparameters between dense FFN and Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) setups.
Overcomes limitations of existing tools (like $\mu$P and SDE) that cannot handle architectural changes or varying token counts in MoE scaling.

stories.title: Good Token Hunting: A Hitchhiker's Guide to Token Selection for Visual Geometry Transformers
stories.author: Igor Gilitschenski
bullet_points: The paper addresses the quadratic computational cost of Visual Geometry Transformers (VGTs) used in 3D reconstruction.
The core problem is the global attention layers leading to poor scalability with input sequence length.
The proposed solution is a general strategy involving restricting the number of key/value tokens used in the model.

stories.title: CHRONOS: Temporally-Aware Multi-Agent Coordination for Evolving Data Marketplaces
stories.author: Joydeep Chandra
bullet_points: CHRONOS addresses three critical failures in static temporal knowledge-graph marketplaces: stale indexing, inaccurate Shapley pricing, and budget over-consumption.
The proposed solution is a three-layer architecture designed to unify the treatment of these temporal challenges while maintaining explicit public/private data separation.
Key technical components include using neural-ODE temporal decay for shortcut edges and addressing distribution shifts in pricing mechanisms.

stories.title: Training-Free Looped Transformers
stories.author: Qiang Liu
bullet_points: Introduces 'training-free looped transformers' to enhance pretrained models.
The method retrofits recurrence onto frozen checkpoints at inference time without fine-tuning.
Highlights that naive block reapplication degrades performance, emphasizing the need for proper loop application.

stories.title: Leveraging Foundation Models for Causal Generative Modeling
stories.author: Xintao Wu
bullet_points: The research addresses the need for causal generative modeling to build reliable AI with counterfactual reasoning.
It introduces FM-CGM, a modular framework designed to integrate causal reasoning with pretrained foundation models.
The framework aims to leverage the zero-shot reasoning capabilities of large foundation models for end-to-end visual causal reasoning.

stories.title: Strong Teacher Not Needed? On Distillation in LLM Pretraining
stories.author: Zhuang Liu
bullet_points: Challenges the conventional assumption in knowledge distillation that a 'strong' teacher model is necessary for effective student training.
The research investigates distillation effectiveness across various teacher-student relationships (strong-to-weak, same-level, weak-to-strong).
Findings suggest that proper mixing techniques can enable successful distillation even when the teacher model is not significantly stronger than the student.

stories.title: SkillOpt: Executive Strategy for Self-Evolving Agent Skills
stories.author: Chong Luo
bullet_points: SkillOpt proposes a novel approach to agent skill evolution, treating skills as an external state optimized in text-space.
It addresses limitations in current agent skill generation methods (hand-crafted, one-shot, loose self-revision) which lack deep-learning optimization rigor.
The method aims to provide systematic and controllable optimization for agent skills, mirroring the reproducibility of weight-space optimization.

stories.title: ETCHR: Editing To Clarify and Harness Reasoning
stories.author: Dahua Lin
bullet_points: ETCHR addresses the limitations of purely textual Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning in Multimodal LLMs.
It aims to improve visual reasoning by decoupling the process from unified multimodal methods, addressing noise.
The proposed solution utilizes a dedicated image editing model to enhance fine-grained focus and view transformations.

stories.title: Strong Teacher Not Needed? On Distillation in LLM Pretraining
stories.author: Zhuang Liu
bullet_points: Challenges the conventional assumption in knowledge distillation that a 'strong' teacher model is necessary for effective student training.
Investigates distillation effectiveness across various teacher-student relationships (strong-to-weak, same-level, weak-to-strong) by varying model sizes and training budgets.
Suggests that proper mixing of language modeling techniques can allow for successful distillation even when the teacher model is not significantly stronger than the student.

stories.title: Decomposing Queries into Tool Calls for Long-Video Keyframe Retrieval
stories.author: Derek Hoiem
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of long-video question answering (QA) by focusing on keyframe selection for visual evidence.
The proposed method, ToolMerge, uses an LLM-based planner to decompose complex user queries into specific tool calls.
This decomposition and merging approach improves upon existing methods that either score all frames against one query or use fixed schemas.

stories.title: ChartFI: Benchmarking Faithfulness and Insightfulness of Chart Descriptions from Multimodal Large Language Models
stories.author: Siming Chen
bullet_points: The paper introduces ChartFI, a new benchmark to evaluate the faithfulness and insightfulness of chart descriptions generated by Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
It addresses a gap in current research where existing benchmarks use simple, homogeneous charts and shallow, fact-enumerating descriptions.
Accurate chart descriptions are highlighted as crucial for accessibility, cross-modal retrieval, and data insight extraction.

stories.title: Why AI-Generated Code Is Always Good Enough — And Never Great
stories.author: harsh2644
bullet_points: The article discusses the practical limitations of AI-generated code.
It highlights that while AI code can be functional and pass tests, it often lacks true quality or brilliance.
The piece suggests a necessary human oversight remains crucial even when using AI for development tasks.

stories.title: An npm Package for AI Agent Orchestration Just Shipped With Its Front Door Unlocked. Here's What the CVE Actually Reveals.
stories.author: om_shree_0709
bullet_points: A new npm package for AI Agent Orchestration has been released.
The package has a security vulnerability (CVE) that is being analyzed.
The story highlights the increasing security scrutiny within the growing MCP ecosystem.
